pi_items.verified_value: https://github.com/lerocha/chinook-database - Real artists/albums/tracks from iTunes Library; synthetic invoice/customer data
pi_items.source: GitHub repository https://github.com/lerocha/chinook-database and web documentation. Confirmed that media data is real (from iTunes), but customer/invoice records are synthetically generated.
Executed ground truth SQL query on actual Chinook database downloaded from GitHub. This is the top result when filtering for artists where Artist_A < Artist_B alphabetically.
SQLite schema inspection of chinook.db: CREATE TABLE [InvoiceLine]. The setup_task.sh uses correct table name 'InvoiceLine' despite task description saying 'InvoiceItems'.

privileged_info_summary: The Chinook database is a real, publicly available sample database from https://github.com/lerocha/chinook-database containing 275 artists, 347 albums, and 3503 tracks. The artists, albums, and tracks are real data sourced from iTunes Library. However, the invoices and customer records (412 invoices with 2240 invoice line items) are synthetically generated. The task requires analyzing artist co-occurrence patterns in invoices, using a self-join on InvoiceLine (not 'InvoiceItems' as mentioned in task description) to find pairs of artists appearing together. The top artist affinity pair is Lost and U2 with 129 co-occurrences, followed by Cássia Eller and Deep Purple with 61 co-occurrences.
